patterns: proxy-envelope client (POST /_api/ws/proxy with {service, method, path, body?})
browser_http transport (Surf with Chrome TLS clears Cloudflare)
metric-rich entity payloads (10 metric dimensions per entity)
publisher verification flag in browse responses but not exposed as a filter

novel_features.name: Canonical collection lookup
novel_features.description: One command finds the best community Postman Collection for a vendor, ranked by publisher verification, fork count, and recency.
novel_features.rationale: Web search at postman.com returns unranked, often-duplicated forks; the AI-agent and integrator personas need a single canonical pick. Requires correlating publisher verification with metric peaks across the synced network — only the local store has this composed.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user asks an agent for a vendor's Postman Collection, this returns the best canonical choice in one call instead of forcing the agent to dedupe a search result list.

novel_features.name: Trend ranking by custom metric
novel_features.description: Rank entities by any metric (weekForks, monthViewCount, etc.) with category and entity-type narrowing.
novel_features.rationale: The proxy's only working sort is `popular`. The metric array in entity responses carries 10 dimensions — week/month view, fork, and watch counts — but the web UI never exposes them as sort keys. Local computation across the synced index unlocks them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When agents need to recommend the most-forked collections THIS week, not the all-time leaders, this is the only path that works.

novel_features.name: Publisher gravitas (cross-team comparison)
novel_features.description: Aggregate fork counts across every entity per publisher within a category; rank teams by total community gravity.
novel_features.rationale: The web UI shows team pages individually, with no comparison view across publishers. Aggregation across entities + category membership is a SQL JOIN that no single API call serves.
novel_features.why_it_matters: API curators picking between vendors need cross-publisher comparison. Agents recommending an integration partner need this to break ties.

novel_features.name: Drift / changelog since last sync
novel_features.description: Compare two synced snapshots and report new entities, removed entities, and entities whose updatedAt advanced.
novel_features.rationale: The website has no "what changed on the network" view. With two snapshots in the local store, computing the diff is a JOIN on id; without snapshots, the question is unanswerable.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents tracking when a vendor publishes a new collection or updates an existing one rely on this; there is no other way to know.

novel_features.name: Verified publishers only
novel_features.description: When passed --verified-only, the browse command filters to entities owned by publishers with the verified-team flag set.
novel_features.rationale: The publisher verification flag exists in API responses but is not exposed as a web-UI filter. Quality filtering is a recurring agent need.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When you want only the official-vendor collections (not community forks), this is the cleanest filter.

novel_features.name: Fork velocity (accelerating collections)
novel_features.description: Rank collections by acceleration ratio: (weekForkCount × 4) / monthForkCount. Surfaces collections breaking out before they top the popular list.
novel_features.rationale: The proxy returns both weekly and monthly fork counts. The ratio across them is a velocity signal nothing on the web exposes. Computed entirely from synced metrics in SQLite.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Catching a breakout collection before it tops the popular sort is high-value for API curators tracking emerging vendors.

novel_features.name: Similar collection finder (more-like-this)
novel_features.description: Given an entity numeric id, return collections with overlapping name, summary, and category — ranked by FTS relevance score against the seed entity's text.
novel_features.rationale: FTS5 'more like this' over the local store's combined name+summary+description+tags index, restricted to the same entityType. Web UI offers no way to ask 'what else looks like this collection?'.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an integrator finds one collection but wants to compare against alternatives, this returns the rivals in one call rather than forcing a fresh search.

novel_features.name: Category landscape report
novel_features.description: For a category slug, return: total entity counts per type, top 5 publishers by aggregate fork count, and top 5 entities by viewCount. One command, one structured payload.
novel_features.rationale: Combines aggregate counts, publisher gravitas, and top entities into a single category-level report. The web UI shows category pages but no aggregate stats; computing this requires SQL JOINs across cached tables.
novel_features.why_it_matters: API curators evaluating a vertical (payments, AI, devops) want one snapshot of who dominates and what's popular; this is exactly that snapshot.

narrative.headline: The CLI for the public API directory at postman.com/explore — search, rank, and watch community-contributed Postman Collections, agent-native and offline.
narrative.value_prop: Postman's API Network is the world's largest public API directory, but discovery is web-only and unscriptable. This CLI mirrors the public surface into a local SQLite store with FTS, then layers commands the website cannot offer: canonical-collection lookup, cross-publisher comparison, trend ranking by custom metrics, and drift detection across snapshots. No authentication required; designed for AI agents and power users who need the network as data, not as a webpage.
narrative.auth_narrative: No authentication required. The CLI uses a Surf HTTP transport with Chrome TLS fingerprinting to clear Cloudflare's HTML challenge gate; the proxy API itself is open.

quickstart.comment: Confirm reachability and see network-wide entity counts in one call.
Populate the local store so search, top, drift, canonical, and publishers all work offline.
The headline command — the best community Postman Collection for a known vendor.
Trend ranking by metric, narrowed to a category.
Compare publishers across a category by aggregate fork count.
What changed on the network this week?

troubleshoots.fix: Confirm the binary is using Surf transport (the default in v3). Run `postman-explore-pp-cli doctor --json` to verify; reinstall via `go install github.com/mvanhorn/postman-explore-pp-cli@latest` if Surf is missing.

troubleshoots.symptom: search or canonical returns empty results after install
troubleshoots.fix: Run `postman-explore-pp-cli sync --all-types` first — those commands query the local store, which is empty until sync populates it.

Confirm `--metric` matches one of: forkCount, monthForkCount, monthViewCount, monthWatchCount, publicViewCount, viewCount, watchCount, weekForkCount, weekViewCount, weekWatchCount. Other names produce empty result sets.
Only `--sort popular` is accepted by the proxy. The web UI's other sort options (recent, week, alltime) return HTTP 400 against the API.

narrative.when_to_use: Pick this CLI when you need to programmatically discover, compare, or watch community-contributed Postman Collections / workspaces / APIs / flows. Ideal for AI agents asked to find a canonical Postman Collection for a vendor, for API curators comparing publishers in a category, and for monitoring scripts that watch network changes. Skip this CLI for managing your own private Postman workspace — that's the authenticated Postman product API, which is a different surface entirely.

recipes.title: Find Stripe's official collection
recipes.explanation: The canonical command picks the verified-publisher, highest-fork-count match and narrows the JSON to the four fields agents actually need.

recipes.title: Compare top payments publishers
recipes.explanation: Aggregate fork counts across each publisher's full portfolio in the payments category.

recipes.title: What changed on the network this week
recipes.explanation: Sync, then diff against the previous snapshot to find newly added or recently updated collections.

recipes.title: Browse top developer-productivity collections, narrow to verified publishers
recipes.explanation: Use --select with dotted paths to avoid pulling the full ~5 KB metric array per record — keeps output small for agent context.

recipes.title: Find accelerating collections in payments
recipes.explanation: Velocity ranks by week-vs-month fork rate, surfacing collections that are breaking out before they top the all-time popular list.

recipes.title: Find collections similar to a seed
recipes.explanation: More-like-this query against the local FTS index — useful for discovering rival or complementary community collections.

recipes.title: Get a category-wide snapshot
recipes.explanation: Combines per-type counts, top publishers, and top entities for a category in one structured payload.

trigger_phrases: what postman collection should i fork for
is there a postman collection for
what changed on the postman network
